plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the single-name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure and reduce overall risk.
Reduce SMH exposure to 8% given its downtrend and to lower sector concentration.
Increase QQQI to 25% for greater income stability and quality exposure.
Add XLV (Healthcare) at 5% to diversify the portfolio into a less correlated sector.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation by cutting leverage and diversifying holdings.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5%/month, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower volatility and drawdowns. While growth potential is limited, it offers a more stable portfolio with reduced margin call risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL as it is below its stop and a highly leveraged ETF.
Reduce SPAL to 3% to lower overall leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim SMH to 10% to reduce exposure to a downtrending semiconductor sector.
Buy LRCX at 7% and ASML at 7% to add high-scoring, quality semiconductor exposure.
This plan balances risk and growth by reducing leverage while adding strong setups.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for ~2.5%/month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while selectively adding high-scoring setups. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns, with improved diversification compared to your current book.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name mandate and free up capital for higher growth.
Sell all SOXL to exit a position below its stop, reallocating capital to stronger setups.
Reduce SMH to 8% to decrease exposure to a downtrending sector.
Increase CRWD to 20% to press a strong uptrending, high-scoring position.
Buy LRCX at 10% and ASML at 10% to add high-scoring, quality semiconductor exposure.
Increase SPAL to 5% to lean into leveraged ETFs within mandate limits.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month, aiming for stretched growth. It utilizes leverage and concentrates in high-scoring setups, which significantly amplifies both upside and drawdown potential. Be aware of higher volatility, potential for rapid losses, and daily decay/rebalancing of leveraged ETFs; strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and reallocate capital towards income-generating assets.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to completely eliminate leveraged ETF exposure and reduce overall risk.
Sell SMH and CRWD to exit high-beta, growth-oriented positions unsuitable for an income strategy.
Increase QQQI to 40% to significantly boost the portfolio's income generation potential.
Buy XLV (Healthcare) at 10% to add a stable, lower-beta sector for diversification and stability.
This plan prioritizes generating steady monthly distributions with a focus on capital preservation.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, focusing on consistent monthly distributions. It drastically reduces growth-oriented and leveraged exposure in favor of income and stable sectors. While aiming for lower drawdown and reliable income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall during market selloffs; this is not a savings account.
